title: teucrium alpestre subsp. gracile
intro: Teucrium alpestre subsp. gracile, also known as Alpine Germander, is a subspecies of perennial herb in the family Lamiaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine to exhibit antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to its rich content of essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Alpine Germander include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cognitive function, and provide antioxidant protection against oxidative stress and cell damage. In herbal preparations, Alpine Germander is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be found in combination with other herbs in supplements and dietary aids, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential interactions with certain medications.
